best_point.rules_reasoning:
Best-ranked site P016 for Tobacco scores 73.6% (good), based on matching weather and soil signals to crop-specific ideal and limit ranges. Confidence reflects how many inputs were present (about 89% here).

What looks strong: Relative humidity (67.8%) is within the ideal band (46.87–70.87) for Tobacco. Clay content (23.9%) is within the ideal band (16–40) for Tobacco. Frost exposure (0.0% of hours at or below freezing) is within the ideal band (0–0.5) for Tobacco. Rainfall (591 mm (annualized)) is within the ideal band (150–1400) for Tobacco. Soil pH (7.59) is within the ideal band (5–8.5) for Tobacco. Soil organic carbon (8.1 g/kg) is within the ideal band (4–50) for Tobacco. Wind speed (15.5 km/h) is within the ideal band (0–40) for Tobacco. (+1 more positives)

What is acceptable but not ideal: Mean temperature is 13.1 °C, below the ideal range (24.94–34.94) but still inside acceptable bounds (11.94–47.94), so growth potential may be reduced.

Data limitations: no values for Surface soil moisture, Sand content, Reference evapotranspiration (ET0). Those gaps reduce confidence and apply a scoring penalty.

top_candidates.llm_reasoning: Crop suitability for tobacco is poor. The strongest positive factor is the zero frost risk, which is crucial for this sensitive crop. However, the mean annual temperature of 14.11°C is significantly below the optimal 20-30°C, severely limiting growth and yield. Annual rainfall (490.56 mm) is also critically low, necessitating full irrigation. Operational risks include insufficient heat units requiring early-maturing varieties or protected cultivation, and high irrigation costs. The slightly alkaline soil pH (7.17) may also cause nutrient deficiencies, requiring soil amendments or foliar feeding.

score_explanation: Score combines weather and soil fit against crop-specific ideal ranges, then applies calibrated penalties for missing features and hard-threshold violations. Score bands: poor (<40), fair (40-59), good (60-79), excellent (80-100).
